english_expressions.meaning: 즉시 업무에 착수하다, 빠르게 시동을 걸다.
english_expressions.example: She hit the ground running on her first day — by 10am she'd already introduced herself to the whole team and set up her first client call.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: New hire starts Monday. Think she'll need much onboarding time?
B: Honestly, with her background I think she'll hit the ground running. She's done this exact role before.
A: That's what we're hoping for.
B: Just make sure she has access to all the systems on day one.

english_expressions.expression: Have a lot on one's plate
english_expressions.meaning: 할 일이 너무 많다, 매우 바쁘다.
english_expressions.example: I can see you have a lot on your plate right now — let me take the client report off your hands this week.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: Can you take on one more project?
B: I want to help, but I have a lot on my plate already. The Q3 review, the product launch, and onboarding two new hires.
A: Understood. What if we push the product launch brief to Jamie?
B: That would actually make a real difference. Thanks.

english_expressions.example: We've been talking about this initiative for three months — it's time to get the ball rolling and set a launch date.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: Everyone agrees the new system is needed. When do we start?
B: Let's get the ball rolling with a kickoff meeting Thursday. Just an hour — define scope, assign owners, set milestones.
A: Perfect. Should I send the calendar invite?
B: Please do. Copy the whole core team.

english_expressions.example: We don't have enough data to decide today — let's circle back on this after the market research is complete.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: Should we finalize the pricing in today's meeting?
B: I don't think we're ready yet. Can we circle back on that next Tuesday once we have the competitor analysis?
A: Works for me. I'll add it as agenda item one for next week.
B: And send the research doc beforehand so everyone can review.

english_expressions.example: I just wanted to touch base before the presentation — any last-minute changes to the deck?
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: Hey, do you have five minutes? I just wanted to touch base on the Wilson account.
B: Sure. Where are we?
A: Contract is signed, kickoff is next Monday. I just need to know if legal has reviewed the SLA.
B: I'll check right now and send you a message within the hour.

english_expressions.example: We really dropped the ball on the client's deadline — they expected the report Friday and received it Monday.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: The client just called. They didn't receive the proposal.
B: What? I thought you sent it.
A: I thought you were handling it. We both dropped the ball.
B: Okay — I'll send it now with an apology and offer a 30-minute call to walk them through it personally.

english_expressions.meaning: 기대 이상으로 노력하다, 한 발 더 나아가다.
english_expressions.example: He always goes the extra mile for clients — last week he stayed until 9pm to fix an issue that wasn't even in his job description.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: The client gave us a perfect satisfaction score. What did you do differently?
B: Honestly, I just tried to go the extra mile. I sent them a weekly update email even though they didn't ask for it, and prepped a FAQ before the launch.
A: That's exactly the kind of thing that builds long-term relationships.
B: It takes an extra 30 minutes but the trust it builds is worth it.

english_expressions.meaning: 관계를 완전히 끊다, 돌이킬 수 없게 만들다.
english_expressions.example: I know you're frustrated, but quitting without notice will burn bridges — this industry is smaller than you think.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: I'm so done with this company. I'm going to send an honest farewell email to everyone.
B: I'd be careful. You don't want to burn bridges — you'll run into these people again.
A: But I have so much to say.
B: Write it all down for yourself. But send a professional two-line farewell. Your reputation follows you.

english_expressions.expression: Bite off more than one can chew
english_expressions.meaning: 감당할 수 있는 것 이상을 벌이다, 무리하게 떠맡다.
english_expressions.example: We bit off more than we could chew by promising three major deliverables in six weeks — now the quality is suffering.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: The new business proposal promises delivery in four weeks. Is that realistic?
B: Not with our current team size. I think we're biting off more than we can chew.
A: But we don't want to lose the deal.
B: Better to negotiate six weeks upfront than to miss the deadline and damage the relationship.

english_expressions.example: Our conventional marketing approach isn't working — we need to think outside the box and try something unexpected.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: Sales are flat despite increased ad spend. What are we missing?
B: I think we need to think outside the box. What if we partnered with creators instead of buying banner ads?
A: That's not how we've done it before.
B: Exactly. Which might be why it would work — our competitors aren't doing it either.

english_expressions.meaning: 행간을 읽다, 말 너머의 의미를 파악하다.
english_expressions.example: Her response was polite but if you read between the lines, she's not happy with the direction we chose.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: The client said 'the proposal is interesting and we'll think about it.' That's positive, right?
B: Read between the lines — 'interesting' and 'we'll think about it' usually means they're not buying. They're just being polite.
A: So what do we do?
B: Ask directly: 'Is there a specific concern we can address?' Give them a chance to say what they really mean.

english_expressions.expression: Give someone the benefit of the doubt
english_expressions.example: He's been late twice, but this is only his second month — I'll give him the benefit of the doubt and check in first before escalating.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: The contractor submitted the deliverable late again. Should I flag it to management?
B: Let's give her the benefit of the doubt for now. She mentioned a family situation last week.
A: Fair enough. But if it happens again?
B: Then we document it. One instance with context isn't a pattern yet.

english_expressions.expression: Put something on the back burner
english_expressions.example: The new feature request is great, but let's put it on the back burner until after the product launch in September.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: Should we include the analytics dashboard in this sprint?
B: I want to, but let's put it on the back burner. If we try to include everything, we'll ship nothing.
A: Agreed. Next sprint?
B: Exactly — backlog it with high priority and we'll pick it up the moment v1 is out.

english_expressions.example: By adopting AI tools two years ago, the company was ahead of the curve — now their productivity per person is 40% above the industry average.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: Do we really need to invest in this technology now? It feels early.
B: That's exactly why we should. Being ahead of the curve means we build capability before it becomes essential — not after.
A: But what if the technology doesn't take off?
B: Then we've learned something no one else has. The cost of learning early is almost always less than the cost of catching up late.

english_expressions.meaning: 핵심으로 바로 가다, 군말 없이 요점만 말하다.
english_expressions.example: We only have 10 minutes — let me cut to the chase. We need a 15% budget increase to hit the Q4 targets.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: I want to give you some context before I explain the issue—
B: Please, just cut to the chase. What's the problem and what do you need from me?
A: The supplier raised prices by 12%. We need to either increase product price or cut margin.
B: Good. Now I know what we're deciding. Let's look at the numbers.

english_expressions.example: We need everyone to get on board with the new process — if even one team resists, the whole system breaks down.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: Not everyone on the team seems excited about the new workflow.
B: We need them to get on board. Can you set up a session where they can ask questions directly?
A: I think part of it is they feel like the decision was made without them.
B: You're right. Let's run a retrospective on the old process first — let them see the data that drove the change.

english_expressions.example: The research team is breaking new ground with their AI-assisted diagnostic tool — nothing like it exists in the market yet.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: We're about to launch something with no direct competitor. That's exciting but also scary.
B: That's what it feels like to break new ground. There's no playbook because no one's done it yet.
A: How do we validate the approach then?
B: Small pilots. Fast iteration. Treat the first six months as a series of experiments, not a rollout.

english_expressions.example: In a nutshell, the project is on track, the team is aligned, and we'll hit the deadline — but we need a budget approval by Friday.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: Can you summarize where we are before the meeting starts?
B: Sure. In a nutshell: phase one is done, phase two is 70% complete, and we have one blocker — IT hasn't provisioned the server yet.
A: Perfect. That's all I needed to frame the conversation.
B: I'll have the full breakdown ready if anyone wants to dig into specifics.

english_expressions.meaning: 기준을 높이다, 수준을 한 단계 끌어올리다.
english_expressions.example: The competitor's new product launch has raised the bar across the industry — our current offering won't be enough anymore.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: The client loved the last campaign and now they want 'something even better.'
B: They want us to raise the bar. What specifically impressed them last time?
A: The personalization. Each segment got a different message.
B: Then let's go one level deeper — personalize by individual behavior, not just segment.

english_expressions.meaning: 의미 있는 변화를 만들다, 실질적인 영향을 미치다.
english_expressions.example: We've tried five different tactics but nothing has moved the needle on customer retention — we need to rethink our approach.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: The team worked hard this quarter, but the metrics didn't improve much.
B: That's the tough truth — effort doesn't always move the needle. What actually changed for customers?
A: That's the question. Maybe we were optimizing the wrong things.
B: Let's map every initiative to a specific customer behavior change. If we can't draw that line, we cut it.

chinese_expressions.meaning: 일을 시작하기 전에 이미 마음속에 명확한 계획이 있다. 준비가 완벽히 되어 있는 상태.
chinese_expressions.example: 他进入谈判室时胸有成竹，每个问题他都提前准备了三个方案。(그는 협상장에 들어설 때 이미 철저히 준비되어 있었다. 모든 질문에 대해 세 가지 방안을 미리 준비해 두었다.)
chinese_expressions.dialogue:
A: 明天的提案你紧张吗？
B: 不紧张，我胸有成竹。这个项目我研究了两个月，每个细节都准备好了。
A: 那就好，自信的人更容易打动客户。
B: 自信来自准备，不是天生的。

chinese_expressions.meaning: 발이 땅에 딱 붙어 있다. 현실에 발을 딛고 착실하게 실천하다. 허황된 꿈보다 실질적인 행동을 강조할 때.
chinese_expressions.example: 成功没有捷径，只有脚踏实地地去做，才能真正积累实力。(성공에는 지름길이 없다. 착실하게 실천해야만 진정한 실력을 쌓을 수 있다.)
chinese_expressions.dialogue:
A: 他刚入职就想做管理层，是不是太心急了？
B: 确实，做事要脚踏实地。先把手头的基础工作做好，机会自然来。
A: 但他好像很有想法。
B: 想法好是前提，但执行力才是关键。想法落地才算数。

chinese_expressions.meaning: 일은 부지런함으로 정밀해진다. 한유(韓愈)의 말로, 탁월함은 타고나는 것이 아니라 부지런한 노력에서 나온다.
chinese_expressions.example: 业精于勤，荒于嬉。真正的专家都是靠日积月累练出来的，没有例外。(일은 부지런함으로 정밀해지고, 게으름으로 황폐해진다. 진정한 전문가는 모두 꾸준한 노력으로 만들어진다.)
chinese_expressions.dialogue:
A: 他怎么能在这么短的时间内成为领域专家？
B: 业精于勤嘛。他每天比别人多花两个小时深度阅读，坚持了三年。
A: 三年？听起来很长。
B: 三年后你会发现差距大得难以追上。复利效应就是这样。

chinese_expressions.meaning: 빨리 하려 하면 오히려 이루지 못한다. 《논어》에서 공자가 한 말. 조급함이 목표 달성을 방해한다.
chinese_expressions.example: 产品还没经过充分测试就急着上线，结果出了大问题——欲速则不达。(충분한 테스트 없이 급하게 출시했다가 큰 문제가 생겼다. 서두르면 오히려 늦는다.)
chinese_expressions.dialogue:
A: 老板催我们下周就上线，可功能还没测完。
B: 欲速则不达。我们得跟老板说清楚，仓促上线的风险比晚两周更大。
A: 但他会不会觉得我们在找借口？
B: 数据说话。列出上次仓促上线导致的损失，他自然会理解。

chinese_expressions.meaning: 이미 좋은 것을 더 좋게 만들려 노력하다. 완성에 안주하지 않고 끊임없이 개선을 추구하는 태도.
chinese_expressions.example: 这家公司文化就是精益求精——产品发布后不是庆祝，而是立刻开始分析哪里还能做得更好。(이 회사의 문화는 끊임없는 개선이다. 제품 출시 후 축하하는 것이 아니라 즉시 더 나은 점을 분석하기 시작한다.)
chinese_expressions.dialogue:
A: 这个方案客户已经很满意了，可以提交了吧？
B: 我想再打磨一下细节。精益求精嘛，差一点点就是差很多。
A: 你总是这么高标准。
B: 客户满意是底线，超出预期才是目标。

chinese_expressions.meaning: 나아가지 않으면 물러선 것이다. 현 상태에 머무르는 것도 사실상 퇴보라는 의미.
chinese_expressions.example: 市场竞争激烈，不进则退。我们不创新，竞争对手就会超过我们。(시장 경쟁이 치열하다. 나아가지 않으면 물러서는 것이다. 우리가 혁신하지 않으면 경쟁사가 앞서갈 것이다.)
chinese_expressions.dialogue:
A: 我们的市场份额保持稳定，是不是可以放慢一下脚步？
B: 稳定是幻觉。不进则退——竞争对手没有停，客户期望也在变。
A: 所以我们必须一直跑？
B: 不是盲目跑，是有方向地持续进化。找到杠杆点，集中突破。

chinese_expressions.meaning: 장점을 취하고 단점을 보완하다. 서로의 강점을 활용하여 약점을 채우다.
chinese_expressions.example: 这两个部门合作是最好的安排——市场部的创意加上技术部的执行力，取长补短，效果翻倍。(두 부서의 협력이 최선이다. 마케팅팀의 창의성과 기술팀의 실행력이 서로의 단점을 보완해 효과가 두 배가 된다.)
chinese_expressions.dialogue:
A: 我和搭档风格差太多了，合作起来很别扭。
B: 风格不同不是问题，是优势。取长补短嘛——你擅长策略，他擅长执行，组合起来不是很完整吗？
A: 这么说也有道理。
B: 最强的团队往往不是风格相同的，而是能互补的。

chinese_expressions.meaning: 말이 도착하는 즉시 성공한다. 일이 빠르고 순조롭게 이루어지길 바라는 격려·축원의 표현.
chinese_expressions.example: 新项目马上要启动了，祝你们马到成功！(새 프로젝트가 곧 시작되는군요. 순조롭게 성공하길 바랍니다!)
chinese_expressions.dialogue:
A: 明天是我们竞标的最后机会，压力好大。
B: 你们准备得这么充分，一定马到成功的！
A: 谢谢你的鼓励，希望如此。
B: 记住——评委想要的不只是方案，是能给他们信心的团队。你们做到了。

chinese_expressions.meaning: 작은 것을 보고 큰 것을 알다. 사소한 단서나 초기 신호에서 큰 변화나 문제를 읽어내는 능력.
chinese_expressions.example: 真正的分析师能见微知著——从几个客户的投诉邮件里，他读出了整个产品方向的问题。(진정한 분석가는 작은 것에서 큰 것을 읽는다. 몇 건의 고객 불만 이메일에서 전체 제품 방향의 문제를 발견했다.)
chinese_expressions.dialogue:
A: 最近有三个老客户问了同样的问题，不知道为什么。
B: 见微知著——三个人问同一个问题，说明文档不清晰或者功能有逻辑问题。不是偶然。
A: 你认为要调查吗？
B: 马上。小信号往往是大问题的早期预警。

chinese_expressions.meaning: 물방울이 돌을 뚫는다. 작고 약한 힘도 끊임없이 반복되면 결국 단단한 것을 이겨낼 수 있다.
chinese_expressions.example: 他花了五年时间一个一个地拜访客户，最终拿下了行业最大的合同——滴水穿石，功夫不负有心人。(5년간 한 명 한 명 고객을 찾아다닌 끝에 업계 최대 계약을 따냈다. 물방울이 돌을 뚫는 법이다.)
chinese_expressions.dialogue:
A: 这个大客户拒绝了我好几次，我有点想放弃了。
B: 别放弃。滴水穿石，你每次拜访都在建立信任，即使他还没说'是'。
A: 但我不知道什么时候才能成功。
B: 没人知道。但你一旦放弃，之前的积累全清零了。
